description: The underlying elliptic curve is secp160r2.
The message digest for hashing the input is SHA-224.
The order of the curve is 161 bits.
Hence the message digest is truncated to 161 bits and not {pbits} bits.
Signatures consists of two parts r,s where r,s are encoded as unsigned, bigendian, fixed-length byte arrays.

references.title: SEC 2: Recommended Elliptic Curve Domain Parameters

Basic.desc: Basic test vectors use the simplest possible inputs. These inputs often overlap with inputs from standards and RFCs. One goal of such test vectors is to simply detect distinct algorithms.

Pseudorandom.desc: The test vector has been generated pseudorandomly.

SpecialHash.desc: The test vector contains a message with a special case hash value.

SpecialPrivateKey.desc: The test vector contains a special private key. The main purpose of this test vector is to check for arithmetic errors such as integer overflows.
